purpose: Small human-reviewed seed corpus for beta validation precision checks.

entries.title: Healthy 3.3V I2C sensor with explicit 4.7k pull-ups

expectedNonIssues.notes: SDA and SCL both have visible 4.7k pull-ups to 3.3V.
Power and ground rails are not shorted.

entries.title: Official I2C IC without visible pull-ups

expectedIssues.notes: Official-complete I2C part exposes SDA/SCL but no pull-up path exists.

expectedNonIssues.notes: Missing I2C pull-ups must not be confused with a rail short.

entries.title: Direct 5V to GND short through a synthetic link

expectedIssues.notes: The same component explicitly ties a 5V rail pin to GND.

expectedNonIssues.notes: This sample has no I2C bus.

entries.title: Generic I2C module where onboard pull-ups are unknown

expectedIssues.notes: Generic module status should keep this as a conservative review warning, not a hard error.

expectedNonIssues.notes: Unknown module pull-ups are not a power short.

entries.title: 5V op-amp gain stage driving a 3.3V ESP32 ADC pin

expectedIssues.notes: The gain stage can drive the ADC above its reference range.

expectedNonIssues.notes: The op-amp sample is an analog range issue, not a rail short.
